name: Graph

unions.name: Graph
unions.comment:
A directed graph data structure.

**The vertices** can hold any data, but you can't have two vertices holding the same data. (Think of it as of `Set`.)

**The edges** don't hold any data, they just connect two vertices. Again, they are **directed:** the order does matter.

aliases.name: Edge
aliases.comment: A representation of an edge between two vertices.

values.name: addEdge
values.comment:
Add an edge to the graph.

If any of the vertices aren't present yet, this function **will add them** before
adding the edge.

Directed: the order of the vertices does matter.

    Graph.empty
        |> Graph.addEdge "foo" "bar"
        |> Graph.vertices
    --> [ "foo", "bar" ]

    Graph.empty
        |> Graph.addEdge "foo" "bar"
        |> Graph.edges
    --> [ { from = "foo", to = "bar" } ]
values.type: vertex -> vertex -> Graph.Graph vertex -> Graph.Graph vertex

values.name: addVertex
values.comment:
Add the vertex to the graph. (Nothing happens if it's already present.)

By default it's not connected to any other vertex.

    Graph.empty
        |> Graph.addVertex "foo"
        |> Graph.vertices
    --> [ "foo" ]

values.name: areAdjacent
values.comment:
Does the graph contain an edge between these two vertices (in any direction)?

    Graph.empty
        |> Graph.areAdjacent "x" "y"
    --> False

    Graph.empty
        |> Graph.addEdge "x" "y"
        |> Graph.areAdjacent "x" "y"
    --> True

    Graph.empty
        |> Graph.addEdge "y" "x"
        |> Graph.areAdjacent "x" "y"
    --> True

values.name: edgeToComparable
edges
empty
values.comment: An empty graph. No vertices, no edges.

values.name: fold
values.comment:
Fold a function over all the vertices, starting with the "oldest" vertices.

    Graph.empty
        |> Graph.addVertex "foo"
        |> Graph.addVertex "bar"
        |> Graph.fold (\v acc -> acc ++ v) ""
    --> "foobar"
values.type: (vertex -> acc -> acc) -> acc -> Graph.Graph vertex -> acc

values.name: fromVerticesAndEdges
values.type: List.List vertex -> List.List (Graph.Edge vertex) -> Graph.Graph vertex

values.name: hasEdge
values.comment:
Is this edge present in the graph?

    Graph.empty
        |> Graph.hasEdge {from = "x", to = "y"}
    --> False

    Graph.empty
        |> Graph.addEdge "x" "y"
        |> Graph.hasEdge {from = "x", to = "y"}
    --> True

    Graph.empty
        |> Graph.addEdge "y" "x"
        |> Graph.hasEdge {from = "x", to = "y"}
    --> False

values.name: hasVertex
values.comment:
Is this vertex present in the graph?

    Graph.empty
        |> Graph.hasVertex "x"
    --> False

    Graph.empty
        |> Graph.addVertex "x"
        |> Graph.hasVertex "x"
    --> True

values.name: isEmpty
values.comment: Is the graph empty?

values.name: map
values.comment:
Applies a function to all the vertices.

    Graph.empty
        |> Graph.addVertex "foo"
        |> Graph.addVertex "bar"
        |> Graph.map String.toUpper
        |> Graph.vertices
    --> [ "FOO", "BAR" ]

values.name: outgoingEdges
values.comment:
Lists all vertices this vertex has edges to.

Directed - only lists the edges that go _from_ the given vertex.

     Graph.empty
         |> Graph.outgoingEdges "foo"
     --> []

     Graph.empty
         |> Graph.addEdge "foo" "bar"
         |> Graph.addEdge "foo" "baz"
         |> Graph.addEdge "quux" "foo"
         |> Graph.outgoingEdges "foo"
     --> [ "bar", "baz" ]

values.name: removeEdge
values.comment:
Remove an edge from the first vertex to the second one. Does nothing if the
edge is not present.

Directed: the order of the vertices does matter.

    Graph.empty
        |> Graph.addEdge "foo" "bar"
        |> Graph.removeEdge "foo" "bar"
        |> Graph.edges
    --> []
values.type: vertex -> vertex -> Graph.Graph vertex -> Graph.Graph vertex

values.name: removeVertex
values.comment:
Remove the vertex from the graph. (Nothing happens if it's not present.)

    Graph.empty
        |> Graph.addVertex "foo"
        |> Graph.removeVertex "foo"
        |> Graph.isEmpty
    --> True

values.name: reverseEdges
values.comment:
Reverse the direction of all the edges in the graph.

    Graph.empty
        |> Graph.addEdge "foo" "bar"
        |> Graph.reverseEdges
        |> Graph.edges
    --> [ { from = "bar", to = "foo" } ]

values.name: size
values.comment:
Determine the number of vertices in the graph.

    Graph.size Graph.empty
    --> 0

    Graph.empty
        |> Graph.addVertex "foo"
        |> Graph.addVertex "bar"
        |> Graph.size
    --> 2

values.name: updateVertex
values.comment:
Updates the vertex data. Does nothing if the vertex is not present.

    Graph.empty
        |> Graph.addVertex "foo"
        |> Graph.updateVertex "foo" String.toUpper
        |> Graph.vertices
    --> [ "FOO" ]
values.type: vertex -> (vertex -> vertex) -> Graph.Graph vertex -> Graph.Graph vertex

values.name: vertices
values.comment:
Return a list of the vertices, starting with the "oldest".

    Graph.empty
        |> Graph.addVertex "foo"
        |> Graph.addVertex "bar"
        |> Graph.vertices
    --> [ "foo", "bar" ]

values.name: verticesAndEdges
values.comment:
Return a record with a list of vertices and list of edges in the graph.

    Graph.empty
        |> Graph.addVertex "foo"
        |> Graph.addEdge "foo" "bar"
        |> Graph.verticesAndEdges
    --> { vertices = [ "foo", "bar" ]
        , edges = [ { from = "foo", to = "bar" } ]
        }
values.type: Graph.Graph vertex -> { vertices : List.List vertex, edges : List.List (Graph.Edge vertex) }
